  conversation title, date MMM d yyyy, access count, delete icon),
  inline delete confirmation dialog, empty state text, Clear all button
- MemoryRepositoryImplTest: add tests for observeEpisodicMemories and
  deleteEpisodicMemory (rowId present + null orphan cases)

  episodic state flow, pending delete lifecycle, conversationTitles map

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#155): address code review issues in episodic memory browser

- EpisodicMemoryDao: add @Transaction getRowIdAndDelete() to atomically
  fetch rowId and delete Room entity, preventing prune() race condition
- MemoryRepositoryImpl: use getRowIdAndDelete() + runCatching on vec delete
  so orphan vec entries don't crash the delete path
- MemoryViewModel: move _pendingDeleteEpisodicId.value = null to finally
  block so dialog stays open on failure rather than silently succeeding
- MemoryViewModel: remove observeEpisodicCount() from combine chain;
  derive episodicCount from episodicMemories.size to eliminate transient
  count/list mismatch on DB writes
- MemoryScreen: hoist DateTimeFormatter to file-level val to avoid
  allocating a new instance per EpisodicMemoryItem per recomposition
- Update tests to mock getRowIdAndDelete() instead of two-step calls

fix: disable Stylix nvf target and silence GTK4 warning

Two HM evaluation errors fixed:

home/features/theming.nix:
- Add stylix.targets.nvf.enable = false
  Stylix has a dedicated NVF target (modules/neovim/nvf.nix) separate
  from stylix.targets.neovim. With autoEnable = true it was setting
  programs.nvf.settings.vim.theme.name = 'base16', which conflicted
  with the 'gruvbox' theme in home/features/nvim/settings.nix.
  All three neovim-related targets (neovim, vim, nvf) are now
  explicitly disabled so NVF manages its own theme.

- Add gtk.gtk4.theme = null
  HM 26.05 changed the gtk.gtk4.theme default from config.gtk.theme
  to null. Setting it explicitly adopts the new default and silences
  the evaluation warning.

- Add { config, ... }: function signature (required for gtk.gtk4.theme)
- Expand target inventory comment to document gtk + the three NVF targets

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: MessagePort per-channel FX isolation (replace broken multi-output approach)

Replace multi-output AudioWorkletNode routing (which silently applied effects
to ALL channels) with MessagePort-based isolation:

- Main worklet renders isolation audio to heap buffers, sends via MessagePort
- IsolationPlayerProcessor receives audio and outputs through single output
- LibopenmptEngine.addIsolation() creates MessageChannel + player node
- ChannelRoutedEffects connects player node → effect chain → master
- Add try-catch for MessagePort transfer failures
- Remove stale workletNode guard that could abort rebuilds

Architecture: Main worklet → MessagePort → IsolationPlayerProcessor → effects

commit.message:
fix: eliminate cross-runtime deadlock — spawn_blocking + Handle::block_on

Replace nested current_thread runtime with Handle::current().block_on()
in all signal consumers (paper, live, alpha). This drives async futures
on the main tokio runtime's reactor instead of a separate runtime,
eliminating the cross-runtime lock contention that caused the portfolio
deadlock.

Also migrated signal channels from crossbeam to tokio::sync::mpsc and
fixed !Send issue by extracting portfolio lock before risk check.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(solvers): add CPU fallback for OOM during linalg.solve

When the 30k×30k normal-equation solve exceeds GPU memory,
transparently fall back to CPU for the factorization and move
the result back to the original device.  Applied to all three
solver paths: tikhonov, normal_equations (Cholesky), and
solve_from_normal_equations, plus StreamingNEFClassifier.solve().

The solve is a one-shot operation so the CPU overhead is minimal
compared to re-running the entire benchmark.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: settings dialog redesign and content cleanup (PR2)

## Settings Dialog
- Replaced QGroupBox sections with lightweight SectionHeader labels
- Each section now uses a bold label with subtle bottom border instead of boxed groups
- Shorter, cleaner form labels (e.g., "加速度列 (IC):" instead of "加速度列 (Inner Capsule):")
- Reduced visual weight: no bordered groups, just flat forms under section headers
- Reset button changed from Secondary to Flat style
- Import cleanup: removed unused QGroupBox import

## Visual System
- Added SectionHeader style to styles.py: bold label, border-bottom separator
- Settings sections now use semantic separation instead of container boxing

## Content Area Cleanup
- Fixed legacy update_progress() method to use container-based visibility
- Column selector dialog: cancel button from Secondary to Flat

## Design Rationale
QGroupBox adds visual weight with its titled border frame, creating a "boxy"
settings wall. Section headers (bold text + bottom border) provide the same
visual grouping with much less chrome, aligning with the modern desktop feel.

## Testing
- All 236 tests pass
- Lint clean

fix(sf2): replace broken after-process callback with reliable setInterval polling

The after-process callback on _producerNode.onaudioprocess was not firing
because the ScriptProcessorNode is created lazily during first play, but
the callback was installed during engine init before the node exists.
With no setInterval fallback, position updates stopped entirely.

Fix: go back to simple setInterval(8ms) polling of sequenceIndex from
C64 RAM. 125Hz polling is fast enough to catch every row change. The
explicit rowDuration from currentSeqEventDuration provides consistent
visual timing via FormatPlaybackState smooth interpolation.

feat(bfs): use FalkorDB algo.bfs for BFS benchmark queries

Replace pure-Python BFS with FalkorDB's native algo.bfs() procedure.
Uses a set-difference approach to get nodes at exactly depth N:
  bfs(start, N) − bfs(start, N−1)

This ensures BFS traversal runs through the graph database rather
than bypassing it. Depth-1 queries use a single algo.bfs call.

Added execute_bfs() to graph_manager.py for reusable BFS execution.

Results: 550/550 BFS tests pass with F1=1.000, avg 0.22s/sample.

commit.message:
feat: zero-based app shell and visual system redesign (PR1)

## What changed

### styles.py — Complete visual system rewrite
- New color palette: GitHub Primer-inspired neutral cool-gray (replacing Slate/Indigo)
  - Dark: #0D1117 bg, #58A6FF accent, #E6EDF3 text
  - Light: #FFFFFF bg, #0969DA accent, #1F2328 text
- New button variants: #Flat (ghost/toolbar), #Secondary:checked (toggle state)
- New toolbar strip styling (#ToolbarStrip): flat, bottom-border only
- New status chips (#StatusChip, #StatusChipActive): compact inline badges
- Lighter containers: subtle bg tint, thinner borders (no heavy QFrame#Container)
- Thinner progress bars (4px default, 20px #ProgressFull)
- Smaller scrollbars (8px), transparent track backgrounds
- Modern tab styling (underline-style, not bordered)
- Menu bar and menu item styling
- Reduced font sizes (body 14px, subheader 16px)
- Kept Spacing/Radius token values for API compatibility

### main_window.py — App shell restructured
- Removed header section (title bar + badge row)
- Replaced QFrame#Container control panel with flat #ToolbarStrip
- Moved badges into toolbar as compact StatusChips (hidden when no data)
- Primary action (CSV open) uses default button style; mode/tool buttons use #Flat
- Replaced boxed progress container with thin inline progress strip
- Removed QFrame#Container wrapping from graph area (borderless)
- Simplified empty state: clear title + one-line text + CTA button
- Graph:table splitter ratio 3:1 (was 2:1)
- Table set to read-only (NoEditTriggers)
- Table grid hidden, alternating row colors enabled
- Splitter handle width 6px (was 8px in old, 2px in original)

## Design rationale
The previous UI stacked header → boxed control panel → boxed progress → boxed
graph, creating a heavy "legacy Qt utility" appearance. This redesign:
1. Maximizes content area by removing chrome
2. Uses flat toolbar strip instead of bordered control panel
3. Applies calm neutral palette (GitHub Primer) instead of cold Slate/Indigo
4. Shows status only when meaningful (badges hidden until data loaded)
5. Reduces visual noise from borders, badges, and container wrapping

## Testing
- All 236 tests pass
- Lint clean (ruff check + format)
- API compatibility maintained: Spacing/Radius values unchanged, Colors API same

commit.message:
Redesign app UI with modern Material 3

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add dashboard UI with real-time active song syncing via useSyncedState (#12)

Adds a TV-friendly dashboard page (`/:token/dashboard`) for controlling
which song is currently playing, with real-time sync to all connected
vote pages via rwsdk's `useSyncedState`.

### Infrastructure
- Added `SyncedStateServer` Durable Object binding and v2 migration in
`wrangler.jsonc`
- Registered `syncedStateRoutes` and exported `SyncedStateServer` from
`worker.tsx`

### Dashboard (`/:token/dashboard`)
- Server component loads songs; client component (`DashboardControls`)
renders play/pause buttons per song
- State scoped to game ID via third parameter: `useSyncedState<string |
null>(null, "activeSong", gameId)`
- "Now playing" banner always reserves vertical space — shows dimmed "No
song currently playing" placeholder when idle, preventing layout shifts
- Accessible `aria-label`/`aria-pressed` on all buttons

### Vote page active song highlight
- `useActiveSong(gameId)` custom hook subscribes to the synced state key
- `VoteSongList` client component uses the hook and conditionally
applies Tailwind CSS `before:` pseudo-element classes
(`before:bg-linear-to-br before:from-indigo-500/35
before:via-purple-500/25 before:to-indigo-500/15`) for a translucent
indigo→purple gradient ring on the active song
- Custom pulsing animation defined in `styles.css` as a CSS custom
property (`--animate-active-song-pulse`), applied via
`animate-(--animate-active-song-pulse)`
- `aria-live="polite"` region announces the currently playing song for
assistive tech

### Screenshots

**Dashboard (no song playing)**
![Dashboard no
song](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/79f2d7c4-9f2a-4d38-9d00-d2c7a3e3c6ea)

**Vote page (active song highlighted)**
![Vote active
song](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/975b3e0b-d1d5-460f-8fb9-4aefda37219b)

### E2E tests (3 new, 19 total passing)
- Dashboard rendering and song selection/deselection
- Cross-page real-time sync: two browser pages open simultaneously,
clicking a song on the dashboard asserts that
`[data-testid="now-playing"]` updates on the vote page without refresh

```tsx
// Dashboard sets the active song scoped to this game
const [activeSong, setActiveSong] = useSyncedState<string | null>(null, "activeSong", gameId);

// Vote page subscribes via custom hook — updates arrive via WebSocket
const activeSong = useActiveSong(gameId);
```
